chili_cercospora.cause: Cercospora leaf spot is a fungal disease caused by Cercospora capsici. It spreads in warm and humid environments.

chili_powdery_mildew.cause: Powdery mildew is a fungal disease caused by Leveillula taurica.

chili_mites_and_trips.cause: Mites and thrips insects feeding on chili plant sap.
chili_mites_and_trips.symptoms: Leaf curling, silvering of leaves, and stunted plant growth.
chili_mites_and_trips.treatment: Apply neem oil or recommended miticides/insecticides.
chili_mites_and_trips.prevention: Maintain proper plant hygiene and monitor pests regularly.

chili_healthy.cause: No disease detected; chili plant appears healthy.
chili_healthy.symptoms: Healthy green leaves and proper plant growth.
chili_healthy.prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization and regular watering.

mango_anthracnose.cause: Anthracnose is a fungal disease caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides.

mango_sooty_mould.cause: Fungal growth that develops on honeydew secreted by insects such as aphids or scale insects.
mango_sooty_mould.symptoms: Black powdery coating on mango leaves and branches.
mango_sooty_mould.treatment: Wash leaves with mild soap solution and control insect pests.
mango_sooty_mould.prevention: Manage aphids and other sap-sucking insects early.

mango_healthy.cause: No disease detected; mango plant is healthy.
mango_healthy.symptoms: Green healthy leaves and normal plant development.
mango_healthy.prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization and proper irrigation.

mango_powdery_mildew.cause: Fungal disease caused by Oidium mangiferae.
mango_powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powder-like growth on leaves, flowers, and fruits.
mango_powdery_mildew.treatment: Spray sulfur fungicide or potassium bicarbonate solution.
mango_powdery_mildew.prevention: Ensure proper air circulation and avoid high humidity conditions.

mango_gall_midge.cause: Insect larvae that feed inside mango leaf tissues.
mango_gall_midge.symptoms: Leaf galls or swelling, distorted leaves, and reduced photosynthesis.
mango_gall_midge.treatment: Use recommended insecticides and prune affected leaves.
mango_gall_midge.prevention: Regular monitoring and removal of infected leaves.

mango_die_back.cause: Fungal disease that attacks mango branches and shoots.
mango_die_back.symptoms: Drying of twigs and branches starting from the tip.
mango_die_back.treatment: Prune infected branches and apply copper fungicide.
mango_die_back.prevention: Maintain tree hygiene and avoid water stress.

mango_cutting_weevil.cause: Weevil insects that damage mango leaves and stems.
mango_cutting_weevil.symptoms: Small holes in leaves and damaged plant tissues.
mango_cutting_weevil.treatment: Apply neem oil spray or recommended insecticides.
mango_cutting_weevil.prevention: Monitor orchards regularly and maintain field sanitation.

mango_bacterial_canker.cause: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas species.
mango_bacterial_canker.symptoms: Black lesions on leaves and fruit spots with cracks.
mango_bacterial_canker.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides and remove infected parts.
mango_bacterial_canker.prevention: Use disease-free planting material and avoid plant injury.

potato_nematode.cause: Root-knot nematodes infect potato roots and damage plant growth.
potato_nematode.symptoms: Yellowing leaves, stunted growth, root galls and reduced yield.
potato_nematode.treatment: Apply neem cake or recommended nematicides. Use biological control agents like Paecilomyces lilacinus.
potato_nematode.prevention: Use crop rotation, resistant potato varieties, and maintain healthy soil.

potato_pest.cause: Potato pests such as aphids, beetles, and caterpillars feed on leaves and stems.
potato_pest.symptoms: Holes in leaves, yellowing foliage, visible insects, and damaged plant growth.
potato_pest.treatment: Apply neem oil spray or recommended insecticides. Remove infected plant parts.
potato_pest.prevention: Regular field inspection, crop rotation, and use pest-resistant potato varieties.

potato_phytophthora.cause: Late blight caused by the fungus-like organism Phytophthora infestans.
potato_phytophthora.symptoms: Dark brown patches on leaves, white fungal growth under leaves, rapid plant decay.
potato_phytophthora.treatment: Spray fungicides such as metalaxyl or chlorothalonil.
potato_phytophthora.prevention: Avoid excessive moisture, maintain good plant spacing, and remove infected plants.

potato_virus.cause: Plant viruses transmitted mainly by aphids and infected planting material.
potato_virus.symptoms: Mosaic leaf patterns, leaf curling, stunted plant growth, reduced yield.
potato_virus.treatment: There is no cure for viral infection; remove infected plants immediately.
potato_virus.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and control aphid populations.

potato_healthy.cause: No disease detected; plant appears healthy.
potato_healthy.symptoms: Healthy green leaves and normal plant growth.
potato_healthy.prevention: Maintain proper irrigation, sunlight exposure, and soil nutrition.

potato_fungi.cause: Fungal infections caused by soilborne fungi affecting potato plants.
potato_fungi.symptoms: Leaf spots, discoloration, wilting, and rotting of plant tissues.
potato_fungi.treatment: Apply recommended fungicides and remove infected plant parts.
potato_fungi.prevention: Practice crop rotation and avoid excessive soil moisture.

potato_bacteria.cause: Bacterial pathogens infect potato plants through wounds or contaminated soil.
potato_bacteria.symptoms: Soft rot, black stem lesions, wilting leaves, and foul odor in infected tissue.
potato_bacteria.treatment: Remove infected plants and apply copper-based bactericides.
potato_bacteria.prevention: Use certified seeds and maintain proper field sanitation.
